text:
The goal bearing is +175°, which is nearly directly behind the current heading (0°). This means the target is almost 180° away — effectively "far_back".

However, none of the provided navigation skills explicitly cover “far_back” cases. But we can reason:

- Since the goal is behind us, moving forward will take us further away.
- We need to turn around — ideally 180°, but our actions only allow 30° turns.
- So we should start turning — either left or right — to face the goal.

Since +175° is slightly to the right of directly behind (180°), turning RIGHT will bring us closer to aligning with the goal.

But wait — let’s check the sign convention. The problem says: “Discover the bearing sign convention by observing your TURN effects.”

We don’t have prior turn observations in this episode yet. However, standard convention in many systems is:
- Positive bearing = clockwise from forward (i.e., right)
- Negative bearing = counter-clockwise (left)

So +175° means 175° clockwise from current heading → almost directly behind, slightly to the right.

However, none of the provided navigation skills explicitly cover “far_back” cases. But we can reason:

- Since the goal is behind us, moving forward will take us further away.
- We need to turn around — ideally 180°, but our actions only allow 30° turns.
- So we should start turning — either left or right — to face the goal.

Since +175° is slightly to the right of directly behind (180°), turning RIGHT will bring us closer to aligning with the goal.

But wait — let’s check the sign convention. The problem says: “Discover the bearing sign convention by observing your TURN effects.”

We don’t have prior turn observations in this episode yet. However, standard convention in many systems is:

- Negative bearing = counter-clockwise (left)

So +175° means 175° clockwise from current heading → almost directly behind, slightly to the right.
